ground_truth.title: 6 Rules for Arranging Furniture Like a Design Pro
ground_truth.author: Lisa Milbrand
ground_truth.main_content: Interior designers have developed a set of rules you can use to ensure you're arranging your furniture in just the right way to look beautiful and be a comfortable space for you to enjoy. Fortunately, the design pros have a set of rules—everything from how close you should situate your furniture to how big your rug should be—to ensure the rooms in your home look and feel as beautiful and comfortable as possible. You'll want to make your design choices based on how you'll actually use the space. People shouldn't have to shimmy past the furniture in your home, so make sure that you leave clear pathways around your furniture. An across-the-board concern for designers: most people are placing their artwork way too high on their walls. Goldberg advocates for placing it at gallery height, about 57 inches high, while Helft varies art placement. You don't want to spread out your furniture too much. So if you're lucky enough to have a larger open living area, use rugs and furnishings to help break that into smaller spaces. When you're choosing area rugs, make sure that you choose one large enough to fit the space appropriately. Yes, it's a little bit of a cheat, but even the most common interior design rules around furniture arranging are made to be broken, depending on the unique aspects of your own space and how you plan to use it.
with: Interior designers have developed a set of rules you can use to ensure you're arranging your furniture in just the right way to look beautiful and be a comfortable space for you to enjoy.
People shouldn't have to shimmy past the furniture in your home, so make sure that you leave clear pathways around your furniture.
An across-the-board concern for designers: most people are placing their artwork way too high on their walls.
You'll want to make your design choices based on how you'll actually use the space.
It's okay to break the rules on occasion to give a space a custom feel and represent the homeowner.
